text.title: Error Budget Policy and Freeze Rules Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Freeze declared; product asks what's allowed; on-call gives different answer than engineering leader.
antipattern.note: Fix: Write doctrine. Get signoff. Publish to wiki. Reference URL in every freeze announcement.

antipattern.description: Burn triggers freeze threshold; nobody declares; budget exhausted.
antipattern.note: Fix: Name declarers explicitly. Configure paging tool to alert them at threshold. Document escalation if declarer unavailable.

antipattern.description: Freeze declared; team cannot ship security patch; CVE remains open.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add allow-list: security + rollback + tier0. Update doctrine. Communicate change.

antipattern.description: Budget regenerated 3 days ago; freeze still on because nobody wants to be the one to lift.
antipattern.note: Fix: Define revert criteria. Auto-lift via check-revert-criteria.py. Engineer notified, no manual decision needed.

antipattern.description: Sales pitched a new feature; product committed; engineering says 'we're in a freeze'.
antipattern.reason: Freeze not communicated to non-engineering stakeholders.
antipattern.note: Fix: Mandate comms to product + sales + support within 15 min. Include allowed work + revert criteria.
